reason: Backend service pre-development checklist and QA service boundaries.
QA service module layout and layer responsibilities.
PostgreSQL/sqlc/goose rules for message and session persistence.
Gateway-facing and internal service envelope, error, and route contract rules.
Classified app errors and stable HTTP error envelopes.

Request logging and sensitive data constraints.
Data flow across HTTP, service, repository, migrations, and OpenAPI.
Search and extend existing QA framework patterns before adding code.
Authoritative QA session/message behavior and endpoint documentation.

Authoritative public API schema and active path definitions.

Go, net/http, pgx, sqlc, goose, and slog implementation baseline.
